title: Josh Isley
content:
People.ContactEmail jisley@mccarthy.ca
- University of Detroit Mercy
- University of Windsor
Josh obtained both a Canadian and American J.D. (Summa Cum Laude) from the University of Windsor and the University of Detroit Mercy.
In law school, he was awarded the Board of Governors’ Medal for graduating with the highest academic standing in his program. Josh also received the Teresa Merlo Memorial Award for ranking first in his class at the end of second year. He finished at the top of his class in seven courses, including Canadian Constitutional Law, Comparative Legal Writing and Research II, International Law, and Transactional Drafting. Josh was named on the Dean’s List every semester and inducted into the Justice Frank Murphy Honor Society in Detroit.
On campus, Josh assisted military veterans with obtaining benefits for service-related disabilities. He was the Senior Editor for the Windsor Review of Legal and Social Issues. Josh held roles as a Career Services Coordinator, Research and Teaching Assistant, and co-coach of the Windsor Law hockey team.
Before law school, Josh obtained a Master of Arts in Political Science with a Specialization in Canadian Politics and a Bachelor of Arts (Honours Specialization) in Political Science at Western University.
At Western, he developed a strong sense of civic duty and began working in politics. As a Special Assistant to a Member of Parliament, Josh staffed the Standing Committee on Agriculture, drafted communications, and presented policy analysis in intergovernmental meetings. Josh also held leadership positions in federal and provincial campaigns. Notably, during the 2022 Ontario Election, he was a Canvass Chair and Volunteer Coordinator in London-Fanshawe. His team ranked top five in the province for attempted voter contacts.
Currently, Josh serves on the Board of Directors of the Child Welfare Political Action Committee, a non-partisan, federally incorporated non-profit that advocates for evidence-based improvements to the child welfare system.
In his spare time, Josh enjoys catching up with friends, staying active, and keeping a close eye on Canadian politics.
